digest.component_or_area: release pipeline and store-compatibility gate for navapbc/rebar v1.0
propositions: Releases must be initiated from the protected main branch via workflow_dispatch with version lockstep across tag/pyproject/server.json asserted before build and production publish gated behind an approved environment, with a v* tag-push fallback that asserts ancestry to origin/main.

A committed store compatibility record must ship in v1.0 so binaries fail closed on present-but-incompatible or corrupt records across all store-mutation and publish paths before side effects, while an absent record is treated as legacy version 0 (compatible) with ensure-registry writing it on next covered write.
Rollback to a pre-v1.0 binary must leave the store operable by treating the compatibility record as an ignored-unknown file.
The published wheel must carry a correct non-null source commit and the exact hash-verified wheel and sdist must be installed and exercised before publication rather than the source tree.

Every release-critical executable must be identified by an immutable reference (full action SHAs, hash-locked build environment, digest-verified MCP publisher) and checkouts must not persist credentials.

Removed env vars and TOML keys affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, or lifecycle policy must fail loud with a targeted migration message and nonzero exit, while genuinely unknown keys keep the forward-compatibility policy.
